species: ANME-2b archaea (Candidatus Methanomarinus) in syntrophic consortium with sulfate-reducing bacteria (Seep-SRB1); mixed uncultured environmental consortium

growth_records.strain: environmental enrichment from push core PC6, Jaco Scar seep, Costa Rica margin (deep-sea methane seep sediment)
growth_records.medium: Artificial seawater (ASW) medium overlaying sediment slurry (1:5 dilution)

up.method: reported (isotope-tracer 13C-DIC production, bulk sediment respiration rate, NOT normalized to biomass/gDW)

growth_records.cite: Guo et al. Carbon monoxide oxidation expands the known metabolic capacity in anaerobic methanotrophic consortia. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-71433-9. [PMC13079720]
growth_records.snippet: At 0.1 bar, CO was oxidized at a rate of 34.97 ± 0.68 µM d−1 cm−3sed, equating to approximately half the rate of methane oxidation in parallel control incubations with 13CH4 (64.63 ± 0.68 µM d−1 cm−3sed).
growth_records.notes: Rate reported directly in text; NOT a per-gDW-biomass rate (units are µM per day per cm3 sediment for a mixed, uncultured natural consortium in anoxic microcosm), so cannot be converted to mmol/gDW/h without additional biomass-density data not given in paper. Organism is an uncultured environmental syntrophic ANME-2b/SRB consortium, not an axenic strain; medium composition (ASW) is explicitly given in a dedicated 'Medium composition' Methods subsection. Incubation temperature 4 °C stated in Methods (Microcosm setup).

growth_records.strain: environmental enrichment from push core PC6, Jaco Scar seep, Costa Rica margin (deep-sea methane seep sediment)
growth_records.medium: Artificial seawater (ASW) medium overlaying sediment slurry (1:5 dilution)

up.method: reported (isotope-tracer 13C-DIC production, bulk sediment respiration rate, NOT normalized to biomass/gDW)

growth_records.cite: Guo et al. Carbon monoxide oxidation expands the known metabolic capacity in anaerobic methanotrophic consortia. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-71433-9. [PMC13079720]
growth_records.snippet: This rate decreased further from 23.21 ± 4.67 µM d−1 cm−3sed to 12.00 ± 0.07 µM d−1 cm−3sed with headspace CO concentrations of 0.4 and 1.0 bar, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Same experimental system as the 0.1-bar record; CO consumption rate decreases with increasing CO partial pressure, possibly CO toxicity. Not biomass-normalized.
